author: @WileHeCoyote
text: very Interesting rob! you really have no idea how much you help directly or otherwise! I think i will implement a similar design in my composting tumbler with 55gal drums of nearly identical construction. i had not yet considered what i would use for a motor, but as it only needs to make a full rotation once a day, under volted and over geared drill motors sound power efficient enough to run off a stand alone solar panel! lol and a sticky rubber wheel should have traction enough in the Arizona sun i would think right?

author: @whitepinelover
text: Get yourself pickling white vinegar 7% or higher like some lands keepers use, 2 day soak , baking soda to neutralize rinse with hose and dry a lot better job and a lot less hassle ! but i enjoy most of your content thanks.

author: @bobweiram6321
text: I would wash off the parts in water mixed with baking soda to neutralize the vinegar; otherwise, you're leaving an acid to continue rusting the surface.

author: @voidmansions
text: Does anyone know where those metal ring clasps can be purchased? I need more than a dozen of the same size.

replies.author: @Zakardis
replies.text: They're called lever-lock closing rings. Since you want a dozen it probably is worth shopping around online for a good deal.

text:
I wet my metal with weak phosphoric acid  and allow to  or force dry prior to painting this prevents the rust  from forming 
This tumbler does a lovely job mate 😀
